channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Breaking: US military strikes Iranian boats and missile launch sites
item.description: US Centcom says the strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".

media:group.media:description: The US military said the strikes were a defensive action.

item.title: Two arrested in Vietnam over Sydney gang boss shooting
item.description: Authorities in Vietnam have arrested two men after Sydney crime figure Lorenzo Lemalu was shot dead in Ho Chi Minh City last week.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Rix

media:group.media:description: Lorenzo Lemalu, who was shot dead in Vietnam last week.

item.title: Tasmania 'just wanted to roll trucks in' in quick fix for icebreaker refuelling saga
item.description: A federal department says the Tasmanian government's proposed interim solution to a costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker is "far from" acceptable.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: The RSV Nuyina docked at Hobart on October 16, 2021.

item.title: Government sets up fight with Santos over gas reservation plan
item.description: The draft framework outlines a requirement for gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their export to the domestic market, which the gas lobby argues will "undermine" the industry.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Mercer

media:group.media:description: Madeleine King says the reservation will put downward pressure on domestic gas prices.

item.title: Farmers hopeful cold front will help WA's dry May problem
item.description: A downward trend in autumn rainfall has WA farmers adapting to early seeding, but hoping this week's forecast cold front will provide a much-needed break to the dry spell.
item.dc:creator: Mark Bennett and Rosemary Murphy

media:group.media:description: With seeding underway, farmers are hopeful this week's cold front will deliver much-needed rainfall.

item.title: Meet the woman who dives for shopping trolleys
item.description: A "tragedy of the trolleys" is unfolding in Rockhampton, where a lack of governance over waterways is creating an environmental hazard.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gamble

media:group.media:description: Cassy Tzoutzias in Moores Creek.

item.title: Israeli opposition leader calls for probe into alleged abuse of flotilla activists
item.description: One of Israel's most prominent opposition politicians has called for an investigation into the alleged abuse of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla, which was intercepted by the Israeli military in the Mediterranean last week.

media:group.media:description: Yair Lapid, pictured here in 2022, called for an investigation into the abuse of activists.

item.title: Iconic North Sydney pool set to reopen after five years
item.description: The pool was first earmarked for a renovation in 2019. Five years and $122 million later, the harbourside pool has an opening date.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Zoë Baker says the North Sydney Olympic Pool will reopen on August 7.

item.title: Winery turning grape waste into 'gold' gets top farming gong
item.description: A winery squeezing value out of every part of the vine has received the country's top agricultural gong at a difficult time for the wine industry.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Becker, Amelia Bernasconi, and Michael Condon

media:group.media:description: Brokenwood Wines has been recognised as an industry leader with a focus on sustainable and circular principles.

item.title: 'Too risky': Blues dealt injury blow on eve of Origin Game I
item.description: The NSW Blues have confirmed five-eighth Mitchell Moses will miss Wednesday night's State of Origin opener due to a hamstring injury.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Mitchell Moses has a hamstring injury and will not play in State of Origin Game I.

item.title: Power price drops for most customers as batteries manage to flatten peak
item.description: Surging levels of renewable energy and better reliability from coal-fired generators are set to give consumers a break, with benchmark power prices to fall up to 10 per cent for consumers and more for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Mercer

media:group.media:description: Batteries have taken over the NEM's most valuable hours, setting the price more often than any other technology.

item.title: Socceroos dealt World Cup injury blow with star midfielder ruled out
item.description: The Socceroos have been dealt a massive injury blow ahead of next month's FIFA World Cup, with midfielder Riley McGree ruled out of the tournament.

media:group.media:description: Riley McGree has been ruled out of the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: 'Inhumane' disability home had fridges locked as residents slept on floor
item.description: Former workers at a disability home in Western Australia's far north raise allegations of neglect, including medical mismanagement, locked fridges and a child being placed at risk.
item.dc:creator: Dunja Karagic

media:group.media:description: Building Dreams Services operated a group home for people with disabilities on a cul-de-sac in Broome.

item.title: Tracing the life and legacy of legendary jazz trumpeter Miles Davis
item.description: From Illinois to New York to Australia, legendary trumpeter Miles Davis helped shape the direction of jazz with albums like Birth of the Cool and Kind of Blue.
item.dc:creator: Ria Andriani

media:group.media:description: From bebop to fusion and beyond, Miles Davis continually reinvented his sound over a five‑decade career.

item.title: Brad Scott sacked as Essendon coach after one win in past 24 matches
item.description: Essendon sacks head coach Brad Scott as the Bombers languish at the bottom of the AFL ladder.

media:group.media:description: Essendon has sacked Brad Scott as head coach.

item.title: Witness recounts 'catastrophic failure' of Vivid Sydney drone show
item.description: The incident occurred not long into Monday's 7:30pm "star-bound" show when almost 90 drones began falling out of formation and into the harbour around Cockle Bay.
item.dc:creator: Jean Kennedy

media:group.media:description: The malfunction saw 89 drones land in the water around Cockle Bay.

item.title: Vandals cut down road signs in protest against lower speed limit
item.description: Police believe changes to speed limits on country roads in Victoria have led to vandalism including new road signs being cut down.
item.dc:creator: Jack Colantuono, Mim Hook, and Zaida Glibanovic

media:group.media:description: A new 80 kilometre per hour speed sign in Gippsland was sawn off by vandals.

item.title: Super Netball record broken by 16yo school captain Eva Carneiro
item.description: The Giants team is flailing at the bottom of the ladder, winless after 11 rounds and the likely recipient of this year's wooden spoon. But their brand new debutant Eva Carneiro has brought some much needed excitement for fans, as the teenager breaks the record for the youngest-ever player in Super Netball.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Carter

media:group.media:description: Giants defenders Erin O'Brien (left) and Eva Carneiro (right) are all smiles after the latter makes her Super Netball debut at 16.

item.title: Live: Oil falls on peace deal hopes and ASX set to rise
item.description: The oil price has fallen 7 per cent on hopes the conflict in Iran will end in a peace deal. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ziffer

media:group.media:description: ASX Board. Bridge st Sydney

item.title: Live: Seven women, 12 children with IS fighter links to return to Australia, Burke confirms
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke confirms the families with links to Islamic State have made plans to travel to Australia after a first group arrived earlier this month. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: A group has left the al-Roj refugee camp.

item.title: Queensland state school closed after students bring in suspected liquid mercury
item.description: Authorities are investigating a potential mercury exposure at a south-west Queensland state school.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Walsh

media:group.media:description: A state school in Cunnamulla is closed due to a chemical exposure incident.

item.title: Tasmania spent $47m on digital program that didn't deliever any modules
item.description: The total cost of the digital transformation program is now estimated to more than double to $119 million, the auditor-general warns.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: Tasmania's auditor-general has found the Health Department's planning, early implementation and governance of the program was not effective or economical.

item.title: Suspicious fire guts converted factory in Melbourne suburb
item.description: More than 50 firefighters were called to battle the blaze, which police suspect was deliberately lit.

media:group.media:description: Victoria Police said people were seen fleeing the scene of the fire, which has been deemed suspicious.

item.title: Bringing Them Home inaction a 'woeful failure', ex-commissioner says
item.description: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.
item.dc:creator: Tahnee Jash

media:group.media:description: Former commissioner Mick Dodson remembers the "awful" stories from the landmark inquiry. He wants governments to urgently act.

item.title: Pope Leo warns of AI becoming 'yet another Tower of Babel'
item.description: Pope Leo XIV urges global governments to slow down the development of AI systems and their disruptive effects.

media:group.media:description: Pope Leo XIV presents his first encyclical, focused on the rise of artificial intelligence.
